job_interest.prior_application_tdrpd_when: Submitted a seasonal recreation leader application in early spring 2023 (around 2023-03-15) but withdrew when my semester schedule changed.
job_interest.types_of_work_interested_in:
Primarily interested in part-time and seasonal positions working directly with youth and families in outdoor and recreation settings. Specifically:
- Recreation Leader / Program Leader for after-school and summer camp programs (elementary and middle school ages)
- Outdoor Education Assistant helping with nature walks, basic environmental education activities, and beginner-level hiking groups
- Front Desk / Customer Service Attendant at community centers, helping patrons with class registrations, facility information, and general questions
- Special Events Assistant for community events (holiday events, family fun runs, outdoor movie nights), including set-up, check-in, activity stations, and tear-down

I have 3+ years of experience working with children and teens through coaching youth soccer, tutoring, and volunteering at a local YMCA. I am especially interested in roles where I can:
- Plan and lead small-group activities (arts and crafts, simple STEM projects, team-building games, and cooperative sports)
- Support inclusive programming for kids with different ability levels and backgrounds
- Help maintain a safe and welcoming environment at parks, playgrounds, and indoor recreation facilities

Longer-term, I am also interested in gaining experience that could lead toward a full-time Recreation Coordinator or Outdoor Programs role, so I am open to cross-training in areas like basic facility operations, program registration systems, and event logistics.
job_interest.starting_wage_expected: Open and flexible depending on the specific position and responsibilities. For planning purposes, I am generally expecting in the range of $18.00–$21.00 per hour for part-time/seasonal recreation positions in this area, based on my prior experience working with youth, current local wage norms, and the cost of living. I am very willing to discuss this further and consider the District’s established pay scale for entry-level and intermediate roles.
job_interest.availability_to_work:
I am available to start work on or after 2025-01-15.

General weekly availability during the school year (January–May and September–December):
- Monday: 2:30 PM – 8:30 PM
- Tuesday: 2:30 PM – 8:30 PM
- Wednesday: 2:30 PM – 8:30 PM
- Thursday: 2:30 PM – 8:30 PM
- Friday: 2:30 PM – 10:00 PM (flexible later for special events)
- Saturday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM (can work full or split shifts)
- Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (available most Sundays except 1–2 per month with advance notice)

Summer months (June–August):
- Available up to 30–35 hours per week.
- Can work weekday day camps between 7:30 AM – 5:30 PM, with flexibility for early openings or later closing shifts.

Holidays and special events:
- Generally available for most major community events, especially on weekends and evenings (e.g., summer concerts, holiday festivals, fun runs), with at least 2 weeks’ notice.

Transportation and flexibility:
- I have reliable transportation and am comfortable commuting to different District facilities within a 25–30 minute drive.
- I am open to picking up extra shifts during school breaks (spring break, Thanksgiving week, winter break) and can adjust my schedule somewhat each semester as long as I receive the schedule 2–3 weeks in advance.
job_interest.unavailable_times_to_work:
I am currently enrolled in evening and afternoon college courses Monday–Thursday, which limits my weekday daytime availability.

Regularly unavailable times:
- Monday–Thursday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM (on campus or commuting)
- Sunday: Before 9:30 AM (family commitments and weekly errands)

Occasional restrictions:
- One Sunday morning per month (typically the first Sunday) I attend a standing family gathering; I can work after 12:00 PM on those days.
- During final exam weeks (typically early May and mid-December), I may need one or two weekday evenings with reduced hours; I will communicate those dates at least 3–4 weeks in advance.

Outside of these times, I am generally flexible and willing to adjust within reason to meet program needs.
job_interest.how_were_you_referred:
I first learned about TDRPD and its programs as a participant—my younger brother attended one of the District’s summer day camps about five years ago, and our family has occasionally used the community pool and attended outdoor movie nights.

More recently, I was specifically referred to apply by a friend, Jordan Martinez, who currently works as a part-time Recreation Leader with TDRPD in the after-school programs. Jordan and I volunteered together at the local YMCA youth sports program, and they suggested that my background in coaching youth soccer and tutoring would be a strong fit for the District’s youth recreation and camp positions.

I also saw the official job posting for seasonal Recreation Leaders and Program Assistants on the TDRPD website’s employment page while I was researching local part-time jobs that align with my Recreation Management coursework.
job_interest.referral_source_name: TDRPD Employment Page (official District website) and personal referral from current staff member Jordan Martinez (Recreation Leader).
job_interest.relatives_or_acquaintances_employed:
No relatives employed by TDRPD. I do, however, know one acquaintance who currently works for the District:

- Name: Jordan Martinez
  Relationship: Friend and former co-volunteer at the YMCA
  Position: Part-time Recreation Leader (after-school youth programs)
  How we know each other: We coached a U10 co-ed soccer team together for two seasons and also helped run a weekly homework club at the YMCA. Jordan has spoken very positively about the District’s training, supervision, and focus on safety and inclusion, which is a big reason I decided to apply.
